module.name: Project Multiverse
module.summary: Foundational research module for continuity worlds: layered, persistent, identity-coherent environments and world-level architectures.
module.purpose: Provide the structural, conceptual, and computational substrate for open research into continuity worlds, world operators, multi-layer world stacks, resonance-aligned world transitions, and RTT-aligned continuity and drift frameworks.

files.description: Defines the mission, scope, and long-arc intent of Project Multiverse.
Outlines the phased roadmap for continuity-world research and development.
Explains how RTT/1, RTT/2, and RTT/3 enable continuity worlds, world stacks, and drift-bounded evolution.
Canonical manifest describing the identity, structure, and purpose of the Multiverse module.
Defines the W-operator grammar for world actions: spawning, framing, evolving, bridging, transferring, and verifying worlds.
Session context block for structural AIs working within Project Multiverse.
Index of foundational papers, models, and long-arc research outputs on continuity worlds.
Structured list of unresolved scientific questions guiding continuity-world research.
Simulation engines and models for world stacks, world transitions, and continuity-preserving evolution.
Onboarding guide for students entering Project Multiverse.
Structured list of student-friendly tasks for continuity-world research.
Contribution guidelines for students, researchers, and structural AIs working on Multiverse.
Definitions and behaviors of AI agents supporting continuity-world research.
RTT-aligned prompt patterns for structural AIs assisting with Multiverse tasks.
Technical notes on how RTT integrates with continuity worlds, world stacks, and drift detection.
